title: Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7v3c5","text":"This is the common Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) protocol followed by the 2023 NUS-Singapore iGEM team. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Vortex the PCR tubes to mix well.

For a 50μL PCR sample, add the following into a PCR tube (follow the sequence): Item Volume 1 KOD OneTM PCR Master Mix (Blue) 25μL 2 Each Primer (2 primers in total) 1.5μL 3 DNA Template 1μL 4 DI Water 21μL

Place the PCR tube into the Thermal Cycler with the following conditions: **Lid Temperature: 105°C; Volume: 50μL A B C 1 98°C 10s 2 68°C 5s Edit: -1°C per cycle 3 68°C 1 minute � 4 Go to step 1, repeat the cycle 13 times 5 98°C 10s 6 55°C 5s 7 68°C 1 minute 8 Go to step 5, repeat the cycle 25 times 9 68°C 5 minutes 10 12°C Infinite Loop (∞)

Upon finishing, cancel the current protocol and take out the PCR sample from the Thermal Cycler. Keep the sample in the 4°C fridge for storage. Otherwise, gel electrophoresis may be carried out to isolate the DNA fragment of interest.
Thaw the KOD One TM PCR Master Mix (Blue) from the -20°C fridge.
creator: NUS iGEM
